title: rubus friesiorum subsp. elgonensis
intro: Rubus friesiorum subsp. elgonensis, also known as Elgon black raspberry, is a subspecies of the Rubus friesiorum species, a type of raspberry native to the Elgon region of East Africa. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds, which have been shown to have potential health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of Rubus friesiorum subsp. elgonensis, such as infusions and decoctions, are traditionally used to treat various ailments, including fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems. The plant's leaves, stems, and roots are often used in traditional medicine, highlighting its importance in local healthcare practices.
